transcript: to the earliest known computer, a mysterious device called the Antikythera Mechanism that kept track of astronomical changes and eclipses. Today, it gives archaeologists vital information about the knowledge possessed by the ancient Greeks. It is in this zone that we also begin to find aircraft and submarines, such as those from the World Wars. Plunging as deep as 200 feet, we can find some of the earliest and rarest signs of human history. Prior to 5,000 years ago, there was a lot more dry land because glaciers trapped much of the water that now forms the sea. Our ancestors spread across these lands, and so on the seafloor, we find their camps, stone tools, and the bones of animals they hunted. These sites give us invaluable knowledge about our ancestors' migration patterns, hunting methods, and technologies. In the deepest zone, no human has ever walked.
